commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds `enabled:false` checks before applying skill env overrides from live entries or persisted snapshots, adds a regression test, and edits `CHANGELOG.md`.

PR surface: Source +10, Tests +36, Docs +1. Total +47 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current source shows the snapshot env override path still reaches strict SecretRef normalization for disabled skill entries, and the PR body supplies terminal proof against the production helper; I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow mechanical repair is available: replay this PR’s guard and regression onto the current `src/skills` paths and remove the changelog edit.

target.title: fix: skip disabled skill snapshot env overrides
target.body:
## Summary
- Skip disabled `skills.entries.*` configs before applying skill env overrides from live entries or persisted skill snapshots.
- Prevent stale skill snapshots from evaluating raw disabled `skills.entries.<skill>.apiKey` SecretRefs during channel/embedded agent turns.
- Add a regression for a disabled `gh-issues`-style snapshot with an unresolved `env:default:GITHUB_PAT` SecretRef.

## Linked Issue
Fixes #79072

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: WhatsApp/embedded channel turns could reuse a persisted skill snapshot containing `gh-issues`; if the current config disabled that skill but kept a raw `skills.entries.gh-issues.apiKey` SecretRef, env override application still tried to normalize the disabled SecretRef and could abort the turn.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on Node 22 using the production `applySkillEnvOverridesFromSnapshot` helper with a stale `gh-issues` snapshot and disabled current config.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
pnpm exec tsx --eval 'import { applySkillEnvOverridesFromSnapshot } from "./src/agents/skills/env-overrides.ts"; delete process.env.ENV_KEY; delete process.env.GITHUB_PAT; const restore = applySkillEnvOverridesFromSnapshot({ snapshot: { prompt: "", skills: [{ name: "gh-issues", primaryEnv: "ENV_KEY", requiredEnv: ["ENV_KEY"] }] }, config: { skills: { entries: { "gh-issues": { enabled: false, apiKey: { source: "env", provider: "default", id: "GITHUB_PAT" } } } } } as any }); console.log(JSON.stringify({ completed: true, envKey: process.env.ENV_KEY ?? null, githubPat: process.env.GITHUB_PAT ?? null })); restore();'
```

Evidence after fix:
```json
{"completed":true,"envKey":null,"githubPat":null}
```

Observed result after fix: The production env override path completes without throwing, does not resolve or inject the disabled skill SecretRef, and leaves both the primary env var and `GITHUB_PAT` unset.

What was not tested: A live WhatsApp message lane was not run locally; the local proof exercises the implicated embedded skill env override path directly.

## Root Cause
Fresh skill discovery already skips disabled skills, but persisted snapshots can still contain a skill that was later disabled. `applySkillEnvOverridesFromSnapshot` looked up the current skill config and applied env overrides without checking `enabled:false`, so a disabled entry's raw SecretRef could still be normalized during channel turn startup.

## Regression Test Plan
- `pnpm test src/agents/skills.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/skills-runtime.test.ts`
- `pnpm test src/agents/agent-command.live-model-switch.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/skills/env-overrides.ts src/agents/skills.test.ts`
- `pnpm lint:core -- src/agents/skills/env-overrides.ts src/agents/skills.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

## User-visible / Behavior Changes
Disabled skill entries no longer apply env overrides or evaluate disabled skill `apiKey` SecretRefs from stale snapshots during embedded/channel turns.

## Security Impact
No new secret source or permission is added. This reduces accidental disabled SecretRef evaluation and avoids injecting env for disabled skills.

## Compatibility / Migration
No migration required. Existing enabled skill env overrides keep their behavior; disabled skills are treated consistently across fresh entries and persisted snapshots.

## Risks and Mitigations
Risk is limited to env injection for disabled skill configs. The regression test covers the stale persisted snapshot case that caused the issue, and existing env override tests cover enabled skill injection.
target.author: zeus1959

commands.reason: repair commands require an open issue or PR
